title: Package and document agent-session-search for clean local setup
description:
Context/background:
The core search_sessions MCP path is implemented. The remaining V1 risk is packaging and setup quality: a future agent should be able to clone this repository, install dependencies, build it, wire it into an MCP client, and run a smoke search without relying on Ben's local memory or untracked dot-agent files.

Current packaging findings from review:
- package.json advertises bin entries at ./dist/cli.js and ./dist/server.js, but the TypeScript build currently emits dist/src/cli.js and dist/src/server.js. An installed or linked package would expose broken binaries.
- npm pack --dry-run currently includes local agent/tooling clutter such as .agents/, .beads/.br_history/, skills-lock.json, source tests, and built tests because there is no package allowlist or .npmignore.
- README.md is stale: it still says the backend is follow-up work, while the FFF backend and smoke path now exist.
- FFF is an external binary dependency. fff-mcp is available locally, but it is not published as an npm package. Official FFF install guidance is a curl installer, so this repo needs explicit setup/preflight handling instead of pretending npm install is sufficient.

Scope:
- Make the built package/install surface real: package bin entries match emitted files, installed commands execute, and package contents are intentionally allowlisted.
- Add a pragmatic FFF setup/preflight path that verifies fff-mcp and, where appropriate, points to the official installer.
- Document installation, build/test/smoke commands, config file shape, source filtering, missing-root warnings, MCP registration, and manual CLI usage.
- Verify the documented flow from the perspective of a clean clone/local install.
- Keep the public MCP surface centered on the single search_sessions tool.

Out of scope:
- Publishing to npm unless explicitly requested.
- Replacing FFF with a custom indexer or rebuilding CASS.
- A full website or broad user guide.
- Project-aware ranking beyond a roadmap note.

Reasoning:
A local MCP server is only useful if another agent can set it up correctly. Packaging correctness is now a product requirement, not just documentation polish.
acceptance_criteria:
- package.json bin entries and build output agree, and both CLI and MCP entrypoints can be executed after npm run build.
- npm pack --dry-run includes only intentional package files and excludes local agent/tooling artifacts, bead history, source tests, and built tests.
- Setup/preflight guidance handles the fff-mcp external binary dependency explicitly and does not claim npm install alone installs FFF.
- README or equivalent docs explain install, test, build, run, MCP registration, config, source filtering, missing-root warnings, and manual smoke searches.
- Config example includes codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes roots plus include patterns.
- Docs state that CASS is not required and must not be run for this tool.
- Verification is performed with the documented commands before closing the epic.
notes: Implementation should be split across child beads so each part can be reviewed independently. Close this epic only after all children are closed and final verification passes.

title: Build search_sessions fanout coordinator and response shaping
description:
Context/background:
The V1 user-facing behavior is one MCP tool, search_sessions, that searches broadly across configured source roots and returns expanded patterns, searched source status, warnings, and raw-ish hits. This bead connects root resolution and the one-root backend into the first useful multi-source search.

Scope:
- Implement the shared search_sessions library function behind the MCP tool.
- Use the root resolver to choose roots and report searchedSources.
- Use an identity/original-query pattern expansion initially if the query rewriter bead is not complete. The response must still populate expandedPatterns.
- Fan out searches across selected roots and patterns.
- Enforce maxResultsPerSource, context, and debug behavior.
- Combine partial results and warnings without failing the whole request when at least one source succeeds.
- Return a clear all-sources-failed error/warning shape, including an rg fallback suggestion if appropriate.

Out of scope:
- Sophisticated query rewriting.
- Project-aware ordering.
- Session-level aggregation or ranking.

Reasoning:
This is the first practical MCP search path. It should be able to search everything quickly even if query expansion is still simple.
acceptance_criteria:
- search_sessions can search multiple selected roots.
- The common request { query } works with defaults.
- sources, maxPatterns, maxResultsPerSource, context, and debug are honored where implemented.
- Missing roots and per-source backend failures are represented as warnings while successful sources still return hits.
- expandedPatterns is present even when it only contains the original query.
- results stay raw-ish and do not aggregate sessions or invent custom ranking.
- The MCP server returns the same response shape as the shared library.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Use fake resolver/backend tests for multi-root fanout, partial failure, all-source failure, caps, warnings, and output shape.
- Add a behavior test for the MCP tool handler calling this coordinator.
close_reason: Implemented search_sessions fanout coordinator with TDD; verified npm run check, npm test, and npm run build

title: Scaffold MCP server, shared library, and CLI entrypoints
description:
Context/background:
This repo needs the fastest useful V1 of agent-session-search: a local MCP server exposing search_sessions for coding-agent session history. The repo currently has a PRD but little implementation, so this bead establishes the minimal runnable project shape without overbuilding. The implementation should support a shared library that both the MCP server and optional CLI can call.

Scope:
- Choose a minimal runtime/tooling stack appropriate for an MCP server. The PRD examples use TypeScript, so TypeScript/Node is the expected default unless implementation discovery finds a better existing convention.
- Add the project/package scaffold needed to run tests and start the MCP server locally.
- Create a shared library boundary for search_sessions behavior, even if the implementation initially returns a stub.
- Create an MCP server entrypoint that registers the search_sessions tool with the agreed request/response shape.
- Create a minimal CLI entrypoint or script hook that can call the same library path for local smoke testing.

Out of scope:
- Real FFF search.
- Full query rewriting.
- Project-aware ranking.
- Beads workflow documentation setup.

Reasoning:
The first useful vertical path needs a process that can be launched as MCP and a library seam that tests can target. Keep the scaffold boring and small so later beads can fill in behavior without reshaping the public API.
acceptance_criteria:
- The repo has a runnable MCP server entrypoint.
- The MCP server registers a search_sessions tool with input fields: query, sources, maxPatterns, maxResultsPerSource, context, debug.
- The shared library exposes one public search function or equivalent deep interface for the tool to call.
- A CLI/script path can invoke the same library for manual smoke testing.
- Basic test command and build/typecheck command exist, even if initial tests are small.
- Documentation or package scripts explain how to start the MCP server locally.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Add at least one behavior-level test that proves the MCP/tool handler calls the shared search library and returns JSON-structured output.
- Prefer testing the library/tool boundary, not private implementation details.
close_reason: Completed scaffold using FastMCP. Verified npm run check, npm test, npm run build, and npm run dev:cli -- "auth token timeout" --json.

title: Wrap one-root FFF MCP backend
description:
Context/background:
FFF is the search engine. fff-mcp accepts one optional root path, so agent-session-search must create one backend per root and normalize each backend's results. This bead builds the smallest useful one-root backend before fanout.

Scope:
- Implement a backend abstraction that searches one canonical root through one child fff-mcp process.
- Send literal search patterns to the child backend using the MCP stdio protocol or the smallest reliable local integration discovered during implementation.
- Convert backend results into raw normalized hits with source, root, path, line, content, pattern, and optional context.
- Preserve backend warnings/errors as structured warnings.
- Enforce per-root timeout/cap behavior so a stuck child does not hang the full search.
- Clean up child process resources.

Out of scope:
- Multi-root fanout.
- Query rewriting beyond receiving literal patterns.
- Session parsing or aggregation.

Reasoning:
This is the critical integration risk. Prove one root works before coordinating all roots.
acceptance_criteria:
- A backend can search one root for one or more literal patterns.
- Returned hits include canonical absolute path, root, source, line when available, content, matched pattern, and context when requested/available.
- Backend failure returns a structured warning/error to the caller instead of crashing the process.
- Timeout or process failure cannot hang the search indefinitely.
- The backend can be tested with a fake child/MCP client without requiring a live FFF index.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Unit-test result normalization and warning conversion with a fake backend/client.
- Add one integration-smoke test path if a reliable fff-mcp invocation is available in the local environment; skip or isolate it if not available.
close_reason: Implemented one-root FFF backend with MCP adapter, timeout/error warnings, result caps, cleanup, fake-client tests, and live fff-mcp smoke coverage. Verified with npm test, npm run check, and npm run build.

title: Expand README for install, config, MCP registration, and smoke usage
description:
Context/background:
README.md currently documents only the basic dev commands and contains stale language saying the backend is follow-up work. The docs need to make a clean clone usable by another agent without consulting beads or local memory.

Scope:
- Document prerequisites, npm install/build/test/smoke commands, and the FFF preflight/setup path.
- Document ~/.config/agent-session-search/config.json with roots for codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes, including include patterns.
- Document source filtering and missing/unreadable root warnings.
- Document MCP registration using the built server entrypoint and the package bin command where applicable.
- Document manual CLI smoke searches and the important response fields.
- State clearly that CASS is not required and must not be run for this tool.

Out of scope:
- Full website or long-form user guide.
- Beads workflow documentation.
acceptance_criteria:
- README no longer contains stale backend-scaffold language.
- README explains install, check, test, build, smoke, FFF preflight/setup, MCP run, and CLI usage.
- README includes a config JSON example with codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes roots plus include patterns.
- README explains sources: all/default behavior and source-specific filtering.
- README explains missing-root warnings and partial-success behavior.
- README explicitly says CASS is not part of this tool and should not be run.

title: Verify clean clone/local install packaging flow
description:
Context/background:
The packaging epic should close only after the documented setup flow has been tested from the perspective of someone cloning the repo or installing the local package. This bead is the final proof pass.

Scope:
- Run the documented verification commands after the package and docs beads land.
- Include npm run check, npm test, npm run build, npm run smoke, FFF preflight, npm pack --dry-run, and a local package install/link binary check where practical.
- Confirm the package contents do not include local dot-agent artifacts or built/source tests.
- Update docs or packaging if verification finds stale instructions.

Out of scope:
- Publishing to a registry.
- Live searches across every real agent session corpus.
acceptance_criteria:
- Documented commands have been followed locally.
- npm run check, npm test, npm run build, and npm run smoke pass.
- FFF preflight/setup command behaves as documented.
- npm pack --dry-run package contents are clean and intentional.
- Local package binary checks prove agent-session-search and agent-session-search-mcp resolve to working entrypoints.
- Any verification caveats are captured before closing the epic.

title: Add FFF dependency preflight and setup path
description:
Context/background:
agent-session-search shells out to the external fff-mcp binary through the MCP stdio client. fff-mcp is not currently published as an npm package, so npm install cannot be the whole setup story. The repo needs an explicit way for a future agent to detect whether FFF is present and know how to install it.

Scope:
- Add a setup/preflight command or script that checks for fff-mcp on PATH and reports its version or health status when available.
- When fff-mcp is missing, print actionable install guidance using the official FFF MCP installer URL and mention reviewing the script first if desired.
- Avoid silently running remote curl installers during npm install.
- Document any relevant environment variables such as AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH_FFF_DB_DIR and AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH_CONFIG.

Out of scope:
- Vendoring FFF.
- Reimplementing FFF with rg, SQLite, embeddings, or CASS.
acceptance_criteria:
- A documented command verifies fff-mcp availability and fails with a clear message when it is missing.
- The command succeeds on a machine where fff-mcp is installed and shows enough information to debug PATH/version issues.
- Missing FFF guidance points to the official FFF MCP installer and does not imply npm install installs FFF.
- The server/CLI behavior remains unchanged except for clearer setup diagnostics where appropriate.
close_reason: Implemented FFF preflight command, docs, and tests

title: Implement deterministic query rewriter
description:
Context/background:
The PRD calls for natural-language queries to expand into a small set of FFF-friendly literal patterns. For the fastest initial MCP path, this can trail the identity/original-query fallback, but it remains part of the desired V1 completeness.

Scope:
- Implement deterministic query rewriting with no model calls.
- Emit exact phrases, file paths, branch names, PR/issue/bead IDs, commands, test names, error strings, stack trace fragments, symbols, package names, configured synonyms, and obvious snake/camel/kebab variants.
- Drop broad generic terms such as bug, code, problem, and thing.
- Respect maxPatterns and fall back to the original query when rewriting produces nothing.
- Integrate with search_sessions expandedPatterns.

Out of scope:
- Semantic/vector search.
- Model-based rewriting.
- Custom ranking.

Reasoning:
This improves recall, but the initial useful search can ship with original-query matching first. Keep this bead lower priority so it does not block the MCP scaffold and fanout path.
acceptance_criteria:
- Rewriter emits 1 to maxPatterns literal patterns for representative natural-language prompts.
- Exact quoted phrases, paths, commands, error fragments, and IDs are preserved.
- Generic filler terms are not emitted as standalone patterns.
- Synonym config is applied when present.
- Empty/failed rewrites fall back to the original query.
- search_sessions uses the rewriter when available and still reports expandedPatterns.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Unit-test representative queries from the PRD and edge cases.
- Tests should assert output patterns, caps, and fallback behavior, not private parser internals.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic query rewriting with tests

title: Implement config and root resolver for session sources
description:
Context/background:
search_sessions must search multiple real source roots while preserving canonical paths and handling missing roots gracefully. Supported built-in sources are codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes. Roots are configurable, but V1 needs sensible defaults for Ben's machine.

Scope:
- Define source names and root config shape for built-in and custom sources.
- Load config from ~/.config/agent-session-search/config.json when present.
- Provide default roots for codex, claude, pi, cursor, and hermes as described in PRD.md.
- Apply source filtering from search_sessions input, including sources: "all" and custom source names.
- Validate root existence/readability and return searchedSources entries with status ok, missing, or failed plus structured warnings.
- Preserve include patterns in config, including Cursor's */agent-transcripts/* shape.

Out of scope:
- Actually searching files.
- Ranking project-local results.
- Creating AGENTS.md or Matt-skill workflow docs.

Reasoning:
Root resolution is a high-leverage seam. It lets the MCP tool search all configured session stores while tolerating machines that only have some agents installed.
acceptance_criteria:
- Built-in defaults exist for /Users/ben/.codex/sessions, /Users/ben/.claude/projects, /Users/ben/.pi/agent/sessions, /Users/ben/.cursor/projects, and /Users/ben/.hermes/sessions.
- Configured roots can override or extend defaults.
- Missing roots produce warnings and do not fail the whole request.
- Source filtering selects only requested sources.
- Custom source names are accepted at runtime.
- Cursor include patterns are represented so downstream search can avoid irrelevant files.
- Output includes searchedSources with canonical root paths where possible.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Unit-test defaults, config override, source filtering, missing roots, unreadable roots where practical, and custom sources.
- Use temporary directories for behavior tests instead of depending on Ben's real home paths.
close_reason: Implemented config/root resolver with built-in roots, config merge, source filtering, canonical paths, and missing-root warnings. Verified npm test, npm run check, npm run build, and CLI root status smoke.

title: Add end-to-end search_sessions smoke path
description:
Context/background:
The fast V1 needs evidence that the MCP server can be launched and search_sessions can execute through the real entrypoint. This bead adds a small smoke path after the core coordinator exists.

Scope:
- Add a command or script that exercises search_sessions end to end through the public entrypoint.
- Prefer a deterministic fixture root for automated smoke tests.
- Also document a manual local command for searching real configured sources.
- Confirm the output includes query, expandedPatterns, searchedSources, warnings, and results.

Out of scope:
- Exhaustive E2E coverage of all real agent session directories.
- Performance benchmarking.
- Project-aware relevance ordering.

Reasoning:
This prevents a false V1 where unit tests pass but the MCP process or CLI path cannot actually run.
acceptance_criteria:
- A smoke command runs search_sessions through the public entrypoint.
- The smoke path uses a deterministic fixture or temporary session root in automation.
- A manual command is documented for searching real configured roots.
- Smoke output demonstrates the required response fields.
- The test/verification command is included in package scripts or docs.
notes:
Test expectations:
- Add an automated smoke test using fixtures.
- Keep live-home-directory searches manual so tests do not depend on Ben's current session corpus.
close_reason: Implemented MCP search_sessions smoke path with deterministic fixture root and documented smoke command

title: Fix package build output, bin entries, and tarball contents
description:
Context/background:
The current package is not installable as advertised. package.json maps binaries to ./dist/cli.js and ./dist/server.js, but npm run build emits dist/src/cli.js and dist/src/server.js. npm pack --dry-run also includes untracked local agent/tooling files and test/build artifacts because there is no explicit package allowlist.

Scope:
- Align TypeScript build output and package.json bin entries so agent-session-search and agent-session-search-mcp execute after npm run build and after local package installation/linking.
- Add an explicit package contents policy, preferably package.json files or .npmignore, so packed artifacts are intentional.
- Exclude local agent/tooling directories, bead history, skills-lock.json, tests, and built tests from package output.
- Keep source layout boring and avoid broad refactors.

Out of scope:
- Publishing to npm.
- Changing the public search_sessions MCP API.
acceptance_criteria:
- npm run build emits the files referenced by package.json bin entries, or package.json is updated to match the emitted files.
- Built CLI and MCP entrypoints execute directly with node after npm run build.
- Local package install/link exposes working agent-session-search and agent-session-search-mcp commands.
- npm pack --dry-run excludes .agents/, .claude/, .factory/, .goose/, .pi/, skills/, skills-lock.json, .beads/.br_history/, source tests, and dist/test.
- npm pack --dry-run output is reviewed before closing.
close_reason: Fixed build output, executable npm bin entrypoints, and package allowlist. Verified npm run check, npm test, npm run build, and npm pack --dry-run.
